title: Flaky Peruvian Rice Pilaf
description: A light, umami-packed Peruvian rice dish that turns rice grains flaky with a hint of tandoor-spiced sweetness. Perfect for a Jain-friendly lunch that surprises with subtle caramel notes from caster sugar.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine the tandoori spice powder, caster sugar, and salt.
Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then drain well.
Heat the olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add the spiced mixture and stir until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the rinsed rice to the pan, stirring to coat each grain in the spice mixture. Toast the rice for 2–3 minutes, until lightly golden.
Pour in the vegetable broth and stir. Increase the heat to bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for 18 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Remove the pan from heat and let it sit, covered, for 5 minutes to allow the rice to steam and become fluffy.
Fluff the rice with a fork, then stir in the grated ginger, lemon zest, and chopped cilantro. Serve warm.
